source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
If in the <image>, \( M(f) \) is the Fourier transform of the message signal \( m(t) \) where \( A = 100 \text{ Hz} \) and \( B = 40 \text{ Hz} \). Given \( v(t) = \cos \left(2 \pi f_c t\right) \) and \( w(t) = \cos \left(2 \pi \left(f_c + A\right) t\right) \), where \( f_c > A \). The cutoff frequencies of both filters are \( f_c \).

The bandwidth of the signal at the output of the modulator (in Hz) is

multi_step: requires interpreting the message spectrum M(f) from the figure and then applying modulation/filter concepts

final quantity is bandwidth at the modulator output, which typically requires combining at least spectrum-reading and transformation reasoning steps
answer is not a direct lookup from the text alone
